ops.body: Closing as already-shipped: the explicit end-of-replay marker landed in release 0.2.0 (2026-07-03, the same day this task was filed) as cc-interact's `caught-up` SSE event — emitted exactly once after the replay flush with the replay's high-water seq (cc-interact v0.11.0 sse/sse.go:217, tested in its sse_test.go TestCaughtUpMarker incl. Last-Event-ID resume and at-head no-replay) — and the SPA gates toasts on it (web/src/stream.ts; no setTimeout heuristic exists anywhere under web/src). iOS decodes the same marker independently (SSEClient.swift .caughtUp). The "multi-chunk replay race" is structurally absent: subscribe happens before the replay query, one flush, marker after. Residual gap fixed in this close: docs/contract.md now documents the marker ("The event stream" subsection — replay framing, once-per-connection marker, stream-plane-only, client gating + unknown-named-event rule). Criterion verified in-browser; evidence on the criterion.
